summary.intent: Address a third round of Codex review findings on PR #687 for the RangeSlider component in the ars-ui repo, fix the reported bugs, and get the PR closer to merge.
summary.outcome: Three Codex review findings (disabled-thumb swap prevention, mid-drag disable handling, hidden input ARIA) were fixed with regression tests added, coverage improved to 95.98% on range_slider/mod.rs, and the fixes were committed and pushed as commit on branch t3code/47fb9329.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot for automated PR review; the workflow involves polling for new review threads, fixing issues, resolving threads, reapplying the 'snapshot-reviewed' label, and triggering the next review pass with '@codex review'.
Snapshot tests are used for component rendering; after intentional changes, snapshots must be accepted with 'cargo insta accept' before CI passes.
CI pipeline runs in stages: fmt/check/clippy/snapshot/mutual-exclusion, then unit tests, then adapter/coverage/feature-flags/release verification.
The spec conformance test 'range_slider_anatomy_matches_spec' must pass alongside unit tests for RangeSlider changes.

code.finding: Disabled thumb drag logic lives around lines 530-565; this is where pointer move blocking for a thumb that becomes disabled mid-drag needed to be added.
Thumb swap logic is around lines 1200-1235; swap into a disabled destination thumb was not being prevented.
Hidden input rendering is around lines 1485-1535; inputs were incorrectly marked with aria-hidden, which violated ARIA contract for form-associated hidden inputs.

workflow: The fix-then-test-then-commit pattern was followed strictly: regressions written first to reproduce the bug, then implementation fixed, then snapshots accepted, then full suite run before committing.
Coverage is tracked per-file using 'cargo llvm-cov'; targeting 'input::range_slider' specifically speeds up the coverage run.
GitHub GraphQL API is used to resolve review threads programmatically alongside replying to comments, streamlining the review cycle.

friction: Multiple polling cycles (sleep 120s loops) were required between each Codex review pass, making the overall cycle slow.
Snapshot tests required a separate 'cargo insta accept' step after each fix pass, adding friction to the iteration loop.
Two separate 'cargo insta accept' runs were needed because the hidden-input snapshot updates were not all accepted in the first run.
open_items: Remaining CI jobs (adapter, coverage, feature flags, release verification) were still pending at the time of the push; full CI green status not yet confirmed in this session.
A fourth Codex review pass was triggered but its findings are not yet known; further fixes may be needed.
Coverage at 95.98% leaves ~4% of range_slider/mod.rs uncovered; the uncovered paths were not identified or addressed.
